heading: Tyntynder South vs Tyrendarra — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tyntynder South, Victoria and Tyrendarra, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tyntynder South has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Tyrendarra (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tyntynder South or Tyrendarra safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tyntynder South has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Tyrendarra scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tyntynder South scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Tyrendarra scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tyntynder South or Tyrendarra?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tyntynder South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
